events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, best known as the lead singer of The Rolling Stones, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Rock Icon's Birthday
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still commanding the stage! Happy Birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger, the iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched in the United States on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the first music video aired.

angles.name: MTV's Birthday
angles.copy: Remember when music videos changed everything? Forty-five years ago today, MTV launched, kicking off a new era for music. 'Video Killed The Radio Star' was the first track played – how ironic!

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which became a cornerstone of the Britpop movement.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: It's been 32 years since Oasis dropped their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe'. A true Britpop masterpiece that defined a generation. What's your top track from the album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York, drawing over 400,000 attendees.

angles.name: Woodstock Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music at Woodstock. A truly iconic moment in music history.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Aaron Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977, and would have been 91 in 2026.

angles.name: Tribute to The King
angles.copy: Remembering The King. Today marks the anniversary of Elvis Presley's passing. His music and legacy continue to inspire generations.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Tupac Amaru Shakur, born June 16, 1971, was shot dead in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and would have been 55 in 2026.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Thirty years since the tragic passing of Tupac Shakur. His powerful lyrics and influence on hip-hop culture remain undeniable.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: James Marshall 'Jimi' Hendrix, born November 27, 1942, passed away on September 18, 1970, and would have been 84 in 2026.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the guitar legend, Jimi Hendrix, on the anniversary of his passing. His innovative sound continues to shape music today.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', on September 26, 1969, featuring iconic tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Here Comes the Sun'.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover, those songs – pure magic. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Christine Ellen Hynde, lead singer of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! The Pretenders frontwoman is still rocking it. What's your favourite Pretenders hit?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, best known as the lead singer of Oasis and later Beady Eye, was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Liam Gallagher's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation and still a rock 'n' roll star. What's your favourite Liam moment?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon released his iconic song 'Imagine' on his album of the same name on September 9, 1971, in the US and October 24, 1971, in the UK.

angles.name: Imagine's Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon gave us 'Imagine'. A timeless anthem for peace and unity that still resonates today.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple introduced the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music players.

angles.name: iPod Launch Anniversary
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, Apple unveiled the original iPod, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket' and changing how we listen to music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: James Bond Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 007 and over six decades of iconic espionage, thrilling gadgets, and unforgettable theme tunes. Who's your favourite Bond?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles, was born on October 9, 1940, and would have been 86 in 2026.

angles.name: John Lennon's Birthday
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His timeless music and message of peace continue to inspire.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: John Marwood Cleese, the iconic English actor, comedian, screenwriter, and producer, was born on October 27, 1939.

angles.name: John Cleese's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 87th Birthday to the legendary John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, a true master of British comedy.

events.title: The Beatles released The White Album (1968)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their ninth studio album, officially titled 'The Beatles' but widely known as 'The White Album', on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years ago, The Beatles delivered their sprawling, diverse masterpiece: 'The White Album'. A double album full of incredible tracks. What's your favourite?
